raw_consumer_statement: My card balance kept going up, even though I was not making any purchases. I was paying {$500.00} every month to American Express and my balance was increasing. I called them to tell them that I had been a victim of fraud and that my personal bank account had been compromised and that I believed that someone had gotten my American Express card information too and had made unauthorized charges. I requested statements from them for several months and never got them. I was unable to log in to their online portal to view my account, and never got help unlocking my account. After about 5 months of making {$500.00} payments every month and my account balance was still not going down, I requested that my card be closed due to unfair interest and penalties. My account credit limit was {$10000.00} but yet they had my balance due of {$11000.00}. I told them repeatedly that I believe fraud occurred on my account and asked for help disputing the transactions. I also asked that they open an investigation into the fraudulent charges, which they never did. Now they have served me with court documents trying to get a judgment for {$11000.00} and have also charged my account off on my credit reports. What can I do to dispute the validity of this debt and request an investigation into fraudulent charges on this account?
